examples.title: Algorithm: Abuse Case Generation
examples.description: Generates potential abuse cases by analyzing system assets and threats
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GenerateAbuseCases
BEGIN
 1. Identify critical assets (data, services, infrastructure)
 2. Brainstorm potential threat actors (external hackers, insiders)
 3. For each asset-threat pair:
 a. Define abuse scenario
 b. Identify attack vectors
 c. Document pre/post conditions
 4. Prioritize cases based on risk level
END
examples.explanation:
1. Asset identification forms base for analysis
3c. Pre/post conditions help define attack context
4. Risk prioritization uses likelihood/impact matrix

examples.title: Algorithm: STRIDE Threat Modeling
examples.description: Systematic threat identification using STRIDE categories
examples.code:
ALGORITHM STRIDEThreatModel
BEGIN
 1. Decompose system into components and data flows
 2. For each component:
 a. Check for Spoofing identity
 b. Check for Tampering with data
 c. Check for Repudiation risks
 d. Check for Information disclosure
 e. Check for Denial of service
 f. Check for Elevation of privilege
 3. Document threats with mitigation requirements
END
examples.explanation:
2a-f. Covers all STRIDE categories
3. Mitigation becomes security requirements

examples.title: Algorithm: Security Use Case Development
examples.description: Creates security-focused use cases from normal scenarios
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateSecurityUseCase
BEGIN
 1. Identify normal use case (e.g., 'User login')
 2. Determine security aspects:
 a. Authentication requirements
 b. Authorization checks
 c. Data protection needs
 3. Define:
 a. Pre-conditions (e.g., valid certificate)
 b. Post-conditions (e.g., audit log created)
 c. Main flow with security steps
 d. Exception flows (failed auth attempts)
END
examples.explanation:
2. Expands normal use case with security controls
3d. Captures security failure scenarios

examples.title: Algorithm: Data Flow Security Analysis
examples.description: Identifies security requirements through data flow examination
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AnalyzeDataFlows
BEGIN
 1. Map all data flows in system
 2. For each flow:
 a. Identify entry points
 b. Track data processing steps
 c. Note storage locations
 3. At each step:
 a. Check encryption requirements
 b. Verify access controls
 c. Validate input sanitization
 4. Generate security constraints for each flow
END
examples.explanation:
3a. Data-at-rest vs in-transit protection
4. Constraints become security requirements
